questions.q: A Galois representation of $G_\mathbb{Q}$ is a:
questions.explain: By definition $\rho$ is a continuous homomorphism from the profinite group $G_\mathbb{Q}$ to a linear group; continuity against the target's topology (complex, $\ell$-adic, or mod-$\ell$) is what distinguishes Artin, $\ell$-adic, and residual representations.

questions.q: Which statement about Artin representations (coefficients in $\mathbb{C}$) is correct?
choices: they factor through $\mathrm{Gal}(L/\mathbb{Q})$ for some finite Galois $L/\mathbb{Q}$
questions.explain: Continuity $G_\mathbb{Q}\to\mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb{C})$ with the discrete image topology (any compact subgroup of $\mathrm{GL}_n(\mathbb{C})$ is a real Lie group with no small profinite subgroups) forces finite image, i.e. factorisation through some finite $\mathrm{Gal}(L/\mathbb{Q})$.

questions.q: For the $\ell$-adic cyclotomic character $\chi:G_\mathbb{Q}\to\mathbb{Z}_\ell^\times$, what is $\chi(\mathrm{Frob}_p)$ when $p\ne\ell$ is unramified? (Take $p=7$.)
questions.explain: On $\mu_{\ell^n}$, $\mathrm{Frob}_p$ acts by $\zeta\mapsto\zeta^p$, so $\chi(\mathrm{Frob}_p)=p$. For $p=7$ the value is $7$.

questions.q: For an elliptic curve $E/\mathbb{Q}$ with good reduction at $p$, the characteristic polynomial of $\rho_{E,\ell}(\mathrm{Frob}_p)$ on the Tate module is:
questions.explain: The determinant is the cyclotomic character, so $\det=p$, and the trace is the Frobenius trace $a_p$: $\det(tI-\rho(\mathrm{Frob}_p))=t^2-a_p t+p$.

questions.q: An elliptic curve $E/\mathbb{Q}$ has good reduction at $p=11$ and $|\tilde E(\mathbb{F}_{11})|=10$. Compute $a_{11}=p+1-|\tilde E(\mathbb{F}_p)|$.
questions.explain: $a_{11}=11+1-10=2$. This is the trace of $\mathrm{Frob}_{11}$ on $V_\ell E$.

questions.q: By Hasse, $|a_p|\le 2\sqrt{p}$. What is the largest integer $a_p$ can be for $p=37$?
questions.explain: $2\sqrt{37}\approx 12.166$, so the largest integer value of $a_p$ is $12$.

questions.q: Let $\rho:G_\mathbb{Q}\to\mathrm{GL}_2(\bar{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell)$ be reducible but not semisimple, with an invariant line on which $G_\mathbb{Q}$ acts by $\chi_1$ and quotient $\chi_2$. What is $\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}$?
questions.explain: Semisimplification replaces a Jordan–Hölder filtration by the direct sum of its graded pieces. The graded pieces here are $\chi_1$ and $\chi_2$, so $\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}=\chi_1\oplus\chi_2$.

questions.q: Why do Frobenius traces fail to distinguish $\rho$ from $\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}$?
choices: because $\rho$ and $\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}$ have the same determinant
because trace is additive on short exact sequences, so $\operatorname{tr}\rho(\mathrm{Frob}_p)=\operatorname{tr}\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}(\mathrm{Frob}_p)$
questions.explain: For any filtration $0\subset V_1\subset V$ by $G$-stable subspaces, $\operatorname{tr}(g\mid V)=\operatorname{tr}(g\mid V_1)+\operatorname{tr}(g\mid V/V_1)=\operatorname{tr}(g\mid\mathrm{gr}\,V)$. So the trace only depends on $\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}$.

questions.q: Suppose $\rho^{\mathrm{ss}}=\chi\oplus\mathbf{1}$ where $\chi$ is the $\ell$-adic cyclotomic character. Compute $\operatorname{tr}\rho(\mathrm{Frob}_5)$ at an unramified prime $p=5$.
A Galois representation $\rho:G_\mathbb{Q}\to\mathrm{GL}_n(K)$ is unramified at a prime $p$ when:
choices: the inertia subgroup $I_p$ is in the kernel of $\rho$
questions.explain: Unramified at $p$ means $\rho(I_p)=1$; only then does $\rho$ factor through $D_p/I_p$ and $\mathrm{Frob}_p$ gets a well-defined image.

questions.q: For an elliptic curve $E/\mathbb{Q}$ with multiplicative reduction at $p$, the conductor exponent $f_p$ of $\rho_{E,\ell}$ is:
questions.explain: Multiplicative reduction is tame: inertia has a 1-dimensional invariant subspace on $V_\ell E$, so $f_p=\dim V-\dim V^{I_p}=2-1=1$ and the wild term vanishes.

questions.q: A representation $\rho$ has $\dim V=3$, $\dim V^{I_p}=2$, and trivial wild ramification at $p$. What is the Artin conductor exponent $f_p(\rho)$?
questions.explain: With trivial wild term, $f_p=\dim V-\dim V^{I_p}=3-2=1$.

questions.q: Deligne attaches to a weight-$k$ cuspidal eigenform $f$ with character $\varepsilon$ a 2-dim $\ell$-adic $\rho_{f,\lambda}$ satisfying, for $p\nmid N\ell$:
questions.explain: Eichler–Shimura/Deligne: Fourier coefficients at unramified primes are traces of Frobenius, and the determinant is $\varepsilon\cdot\chi^{k-1}$ (the cyclotomic character raised to the $k-1$ power, times the nebentypus).

questions.q: A residual representation $\bar\rho:G_\mathbb{Q}\to\mathrm{GL}_2(\bar{\mathbb{F}}_\ell)$ is called modular if:
choices: it arises as the reduction mod $\ell$ of some $\rho_{f,\lambda}$ for a cuspidal eigenform $f$
questions.explain: Modularity of a residual (mod-$\ell$) representation means it comes from a cuspidal eigenform $f$ via $\bar\rho\cong\bar\rho_{f,\lambda}$. Modularity lifting theorems ($R=T$) propagate modularity from $\bar\rho$ to its $\ell$-adic lifts.

questions.q: The weight-12 cusp form $\Delta=\sum\tau(n)q^n$ has $\tau(2)=-24$. By Deligne, what is $\operatorname{tr}\rho_{\Delta,\ell}(\mathrm{Frob}_2)$ for $\ell\ne 2$?
questions.explain: Deligne's construction gives $\operatorname{tr}\rho_{\Delta,\ell}(\mathrm{Frob}_p)=\tau(p)$, so the trace at $p=2$ is $\tau(2)=-24$.
